description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a sturdy frame. The solar panels are elevated above a base structure that appears to house electrical components, possibly including an inverter and battery storage. The base is equipped with visible control panels, vents, and colored elements that may indicate access points or safety features. The frame supporting the solar panels is robust, likely made of metal, and designed to keep the panels at a fixed tilt for optimal sunlight exposure. The overall design is modular and suitable for installation in urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or small utility areas. The color scheme is primarily dark with some contrasting accents, and the surfaces appear hard and smooth, typical of industrial-grade solar equipment.
description: A compact solar energy unit with a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a metal frame above a base housing electrical components. The design is modular, robust, and features a dark color scheme with some colored accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the solar panel array elevated above a base with visible control panels and colored elements.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the base and the tilt of the solar panels, with some colored access points visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panel array and the full width of the base structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the angled surface of the base and additional control or access features.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a robust metal (likely aluminum or steel) frame
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of 4 rectangular panels mounted side by side
